title: Kangaroo Level 3-4 - Geometry Drill
disclaimer: Original practice problems inspired by Math Kangaroo format. Not official AKSF content.

questions.question_text: How many sides does a regular hexagon have?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 100 100' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><polygon points='50,10 85,30 85,70 50,90 15,70 15,30' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A regular hexagon.
questions.solution: A hexagon has 6 sides by definition.

questions.question_text: Which shape has exactly three corners?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 60' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='5' y='10' width='30' height='30' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><polygon points='60,40 75,10 90,40' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><circle cx='125' cy='25' r='15' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><polygon points='155,10 185,10 190,40 150,40' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A square, a triangle, a circle, and a trapezoid shown side by side.
questions.solution: A triangle is the polygon with exactly three corners (vertices).

questions.question_text: A square has a side length of 5 cm. What is its perimeter?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 100 100' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='20' y='20' width='60' height='60' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><text x='50' y='15' text-anchor='middle' font-size='10' fill='currentColor'>5 cm</text></svg>
questions.image_description: A square labeled 5 cm on one side.
questions.solution: Perimeter of a square = 4 times side = 4 x 5 = 20 cm.

questions.question_text: How many right angles does a rectangle have?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 120 80' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='10' y='15' width='100' height='50' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A rectangle.
questions.solution: A rectangle has 4 corners, and each corner is a right angle.

questions.question_text: Count the number of triangles in the picture.
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 120 80' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><polygon points='10,70 40,10 70,70' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><polygon points='60,70 85,20 110,70' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='40' y1='10' x2='40' y2='70' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/></svg>
questions.image_description: Two triangles, the left one split down the middle by a vertical line.
questions.solution: The left triangle is split into 2 small triangles, plus the right triangle, giving 3 triangles total.

questions.question_text: Which letter has a vertical line of symmetry?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 40' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><text x='10' y='30' font-size='28' fill='currentColor'>F</text><text x='50' y='30' font-size='28' fill='currentColor'>A</text><text x='90' y='30' font-size='28' fill='currentColor'>L</text><text x='130' y='30' font-size='28' fill='currentColor'>R</text><text x='170' y='30' font-size='28' fill='currentColor'>P</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Letters F, A, L, R, P shown in a row.
questions.solution: The letter A is symmetric when folded along a vertical line through its center.

questions.question_text: A rectangle has length 6 cm and width 3 cm. What is its perimeter?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 140 90' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='20' y='25' width='100' height='50' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><text x='70' y='20' text-anchor='middle' font-size='10' fill='currentColor'>6 cm</text><text x='130' y='55' font-size='10' fill='currentColor'>3 cm</text></svg>
questions.image_description: A rectangle labeled 6 cm long and 3 cm wide.
questions.solution: Perimeter = 2 x (6 + 3) = 2 x 9 = 18 cm.

questions.question_text: How many vertices (corners) does a pentagon have?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 100 100' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><polygon points='50,10 90,40 75,85 25,85 10,40' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A regular pentagon.
questions.solution: A pentagon is a polygon with 5 vertices.

questions.question_text: The figure is made from unit squares. What is its area in square units?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 120 90' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><g fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><rect x='10' y='10' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='30' y='10' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='50' y='10' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='10' y='30' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='30' y='30' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='10' y='50' width='20' height='20'/></g></svg>
questions.image_description: An L-shaped figure made of 6 unit squares.
questions.solution: Counting the unit squares gives 6.

questions.question_text: A square piece of paper is folded exactly in half. Which shape can NOT appear?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 60' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><g fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><rect x='10' y='15' width='30' height='30'/><rect x='55' y='15' width='40' height='20'/><polygon points='110,45 140,45 125,15'/><circle cx='170' cy='30' r='15'/><rect x='195' y='15' width='20' height='30'/></g></svg>
questions.image_description: Five shapes: small square, thin rectangle, triangle, circle, tall rectangle.
questions.solution: Folding a square along a line gives a rectangle, a smaller square, or a triangle. A circle cannot be formed by folding a square.

questions.question_text: How many lines of symmetry does a square have?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 100 100' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='20' y='20' width='60' height='60' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='50' y1='20' x2='50' y2='80' stroke='currentColor' stroke-dasharray='3 2'/><line x1='20' y1='50' x2='80' y2='50' stroke='currentColor' stroke-dasharray='3 2'/><line x1='20' y1='20' x2='80' y2='80' stroke='currentColor' stroke-dasharray='3 2'/><line x1='80' y1='20' x2='20' y2='80' stroke='currentColor' stroke-dasharray='3 2'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A square with dashed lines showing possible symmetry axes.
questions.solution: A square has 4 lines of symmetry: 2 diagonals and 2 through the midpoints of opposite sides.

questions.question_text: A rectangle is 8 cm long and 2 cm wide. What is its area?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 160 60' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='15' y='15' width='120' height='30' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><text x='75' y='10' text-anchor='middle' font-size='10' fill='currentColor'>8 cm</text><text x='140' y='35' font-size='10' fill='currentColor'>2 cm</text></svg>
questions.image_description: A long thin rectangle labeled 8 cm by 2 cm.
questions.solution: Area = length x width = 8 x 2 = 16 square cm.

questions.question_text: How many small triangles make up the larger triangle shown?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 120 110' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><g fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><polygon points='60,10 10,100 110,100'/><line x1='35' y1='55' x2='85' y2='55'/><line x1='35' y1='55' x2='60' y2='100'/><line x1='85' y1='55' x2='60' y2='100'/></g></svg>
questions.image_description: A large triangle divided into 4 small triangles by joining midpoints of sides.
questions.solution: Connecting the midpoints of the sides of a triangle splits it into 4 smaller congruent triangles.

questions.question_text: Which solid has 6 square faces?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 70' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><g fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><rect x='10' y='15' width='30' height='30'/><polyline points='10,15 20,5 50,5 50,35 40,45'/><line x1='40' y1='15' x2='50' y2='5'/><circle cx='80' cy='30' r='15'/><polygon points='120,45 150,45 135,15'/><polygon points='175,45 205,45 215,35 215,10 190,10 175,25'/><polygon points='225,45 255,25 240,10 225,20' transform='translate(-5,0)'/></g></svg>
questions.image_description: Several solids: a cube, a sphere, a triangular pyramid, and others.
questions.solution: A cube has exactly 6 square faces.

questions.question_text: A triangle has sides 5 cm, 7 cm, and 9 cm. What is its perimeter?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 140 100' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><polygon points='20,80 120,80 50,15' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><text x='70' y='95' text-anchor='middle' font-size='10' fill='currentColor'>9 cm</text><text x='20' y='45' font-size='10' fill='currentColor'>5 cm</text><text x='95' y='45' font-size='10' fill='currentColor'>7 cm</text></svg>
questions.image_description: A triangle with sides labeled 5, 7, and 9 cm.
questions.solution: Perimeter = 5 + 7 + 9 = 21 cm.

questions.question_text: How many edges does a cube have?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 100 100' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><g fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><rect x='20' y='30' width='50' height='50'/><polyline points='20,30 35,15 85,15 85,65 70,80'/><line x1='70' y1='30' x2='85' y2='15'/></g></svg>
questions.image_description: A cube drawn in 3D.
questions.solution: A cube has 12 edges: 4 top, 4 bottom, and 4 vertical.

questions.question_text: A square of side 6 cm is divided into 9 equal small squares. What is the area of one small square?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 100 100' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><g fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><rect x='20' y='20' width='60' height='60'/><line x1='40' y1='20' x2='40' y2='80'/><line x1='60' y1='20' x2='60' y2='80'/><line x1='20' y1='40' x2='80' y2='40'/><line x1='20' y1='60' x2='80' y2='60'/></g></svg>
questions.image_description: A 6 cm square partitioned into a 3x3 grid of small squares.
questions.solution: Each small square has side 6 / 3 = 2 cm, so area = 2 x 2 = 4 sq cm.

questions.question_text: A rectangle has a perimeter of 20 cm and a length of 7 cm. What is its width?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 160 80' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='15' y='20' width='130' height='40' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><text x='80' y='15' text-anchor='middle' font-size='10' fill='currentColor'>7 cm</text><text x='150' y='45' font-size='10' fill='currentColor'>?</text></svg>
questions.image_description: A rectangle with length 7 cm and unknown width.
questions.solution: Half the perimeter is 10 cm = length + width, so width = 10 - 7 = 3 cm.

questions.question_text: How many squares of all sizes can you count in a 2 by 2 grid?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 100 100' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><g fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><rect x='20' y='20' width='60' height='60'/><line x1='50' y1='20' x2='50' y2='80'/><line x1='20' y1='50' x2='80' y2='50'/></g></svg>
questions.image_description: A 2x2 grid of unit squares forming a larger square.
questions.solution: There are 4 unit squares plus 1 big square = 5 squares total.

questions.question_text: A shape is made by putting two identical squares of side 3 cm next to each other. What is the perimeter of the resulting rectangle?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 160 70' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><g fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><rect x='20' y='15' width='40' height='40'/><rect x='60' y='15' width='40' height='40'/></g></svg>
questions.image_description: Two 3 cm squares joined edge to edge forming a 6 by 3 rectangle.
questions.solution: The rectangle is 6 cm by 3 cm, so perimeter = 2 x (6 + 3) = 18 cm.

questions.question_text: Which polygon below has more than 4 lines of symmetry?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 260 70' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><g fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><rect x='10' y='20' width='30' height='30'/><polygon points='60,50 90,50 75,20'/><polygon points='110,50 140,50 145,30 125,15 105,30'/><polygon points='160,50 200,50 210,35 200,20 160,20 150,35'/><circle cx='235' cy='35' r='15'/></g></svg>
questions.image_description: A square, a triangle, a pentagon, a hexagon, and a circle.
questions.solution: A regular hexagon has 6 lines of symmetry, which is more than 4.

questions.question_text: A floor is tiled with 5 rows of 4 square tiles. Each tile is 2 cm on a side. What is the total area of the floor?
questions.image_description: A grid showing 5 rows and 4 columns of square tiles.
questions.solution: There are 5 x 4 = 20 tiles. Each tile has area 2 x 2 = 4 sq cm. Total = 20 x 4 = 80 sq cm.

questions.question_text: A square garden has a perimeter of 32 m. What is its area?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 100 100' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='15' y='15' width='70' height='70' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><text x='50' y='55' text-anchor='middle' font-size='10' fill='currentColor'>P = 32 m</text></svg>
questions.image_description: A square garden with perimeter labeled 32 m.
questions.solution: Side = 32 / 4 = 8 m. Area = 8 x 8 = 64 sq m.

questions.question_text: A big rectangle 10 cm by 6 cm has a smaller 4 cm by 3 cm rectangle cut out of one corner. What is the area of the remaining shape?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 160 110' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><g fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><polyline points='20,20 120,20 120,50 80,50 80,80 20,80 20,20'/></g></svg>
questions.image_description: An L-shaped region formed by a 10x6 rectangle with a 4x3 corner removed.
questions.solution: Big area = 10 x 6 = 60 sq cm. Cut out = 4 x 3 = 12 sq cm. Remaining = 60 - 12 = 48 sq cm.
